content.text: Spark master is healthy. Now I'll wait for the baseline job to complete by polling it.

input.command:
echo "Waiting for baseline job to complete..."
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job spark-skew-baseline -n spark-skew -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[0].type}' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "$STATUS" = "Complete" ]; then
    echo "Baseline job COMPLETED after ${i} * 10s"
    break
  elif [ "$STATUS" = "Failed" ]; then
    echo "Baseline job FAILED"
    break
  fi
  echo "  iteration $i: status=$STATUS, waiting..."
  sleep 10
done
kubectl get job spark-skew-baseline -n spark-skew 2>&1

content.text: Both optimization YAML files are available. Let me apply them both now.
Both jobs created. Now I'll poll until both complete.

input.command:
echo "Polling for broadcast and AQE jobs to complete..."
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  B_SUCC=$(kubectl get job spark-skew-broadcast -n spark-skew -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}' 2>/dev/null)
  B_FAIL=$(kubectl get job spark-skew-broadcast -n spark-skew -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>/dev/null)
  A_SUCC=$(kubectl get job spark-skew-aqe -n spark-skew -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}' 2>/dev/null)
  A_FAIL=$(kubectl get job spark-skew-aqe -n spark-skew -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "  iter $i: broadcast(succ=$B_SUCC fail=$B_FAIL) aqe(succ=$A_SUCC fail=$A_FAIL)"
  if [ "${B_SUCC:-0}" = "1" ] && [ "${A_SUCC:-0}" = "1" ]; then
    echo "Both jobs COMPLETED!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
kubectl get jobs -n spark-skew 2>&1
